title: primula viscosa subsp. commutata
intro: Primula viscosa subsp. commutata, also known as sticky primrose, is a plant species in the Primulaceae family, characterized by its sticky leaves and yellow flowers. The medicinal properties of Primula viscosa subsp. commutata are attributed to its saponin, flavonoid, and phenolic acid content, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Primula viscosa subsp. commutata are often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
